intent: Stop the on-device phone_focus_mode enforcer from overheating the phone by eliminating redundant per-tick firewall/rule rebuilds, while preserving self-healing enforcement; also whitelist the new wake_alarm_sync app so it can set tomorrow's alarm at night.
scope: Non-goal: replacing the interim iptables stopgap with a proper netd/eBPF per-UID firewall (tracked as a follow-up)
changes: config.sh: throttle CURFEW_NET_REASSERT_INTERVAL 1s -> 15s. A 1s interval forked `iptables -L` every second all night, pegging netd at ~30% CPU and overheating the phone; 15s matches HOSTS/LAUNCHER pacing. Deliberate tradeoff: curfew leak window grows from <=1s to <=15s.
config.sh: add com.kuhy.wake_alarm_sync to the general and curfew-night app whitelists so the wake-alarm phone companion can run (it writes the alarm time to a GitHub Gist).

dns_enforcer.sh: state-gate the DNS chain rebuild. fill_chain_v4/v6 flush+reinsert ~50-100 rules each call; running that unconditionally every tick pegged netd at ~50% CPU on the xtables lock even though the rule set never changes. New chain_intact()/expected_rule_count() only rebuild when the chain is missing, untethered from OUTPUT, or the wrong size; self-healing is preserved.
focus_daemon.sh: build the blocked-system-apps file once at startup instead of every enable sweep, and memoize is_allowed() lookups once per main-loop tick.
deploy.sh: drop `su --mount-master` (removed in Magisk v26+); plain `su -c` runs in the correct mount namespace for our use cases.
SKILL.md: document the state-change-gate pattern (only rebuild firewall rules when tampered) in the efficient-polling-scripts skill.

verification.evidence: shellcheck, no-polling-antipatterns, codespell and generic hooks pass

risks: Curfew net leak window grows from <=1s to <=15s during the reassert interval; acceptable vs. thermal/CPU cost.
State-gate on the DNS chain relies on chain_intact() correctly detecting tampering (existence + attachment + rule count); a subtle mismatch that always reads intact would skip a legitimate rebuild.
Dropping --mount-master assumes the deployed Magisk is v26+.
rollback: git revert the commit to restore unconditional rebuilds and the 1s interval.
After rollback, re-run deploy.sh and confirm the DNS/curfew chains reassert and the whitelist no longer contains wake_alarm_sync.
